mnemonics: Pendants are like Puppets, often alone
Null graphs are like Empty rooms, nothing to show
keyPoints: A pendant vertex is a vertex with degree 1, connected to only one edge.
A null graph is a graph with no edges, essentially an empty graph.
Pendant vertices can be thought of as individual nodes without any connections to other nodes.
Null graphs do not have any connections between nodes, making them essentially disconnected.
